an4.94:1.1: "Bhikkhus, these four persons are present and found in the world.
an4.94:1.3: Here, bhikkhus, one person gains inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*], but does not gain insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*].
an4.94:1.4: And here, bhikkhus, one person gains insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*], but does not gain inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*].
an4.94:1.5: And here, bhikkhus, one person gains neither inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*] nor insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*].
an4.94:1.6: And here, bhikkhus, one person gains both inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*] and insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*].
an4.94:2.1: Now, bhikkhus, as to the person who gains inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*] but does not gain insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*], that person should approach the person who gains insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*] and say to him:
an4.94:2.4: How should *saб№…khДҒras* be seen with insight [*vipassitabbДҒ*]?вҖҷ
an4.94:2.5: He explains it to him according to what he has seen and known:
an4.94:2.6: вҖҳThis is how, friend, *saб№…khДҒras* should be seen, this is how *saб№…khДҒras* should be examined, this is how *saб№…khДҒras* should be seen with insight [*vipassitabbДҒ*].вҖҷ
an4.94:2.7: Then after some time he gains both inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*] and insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*].
an4.94:3.1: Now, bhikkhus, as to the person who gains insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*] but does not gain inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*], that person should approach the person who gains inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*] and say to him:
an4.94:3.2: вҖҳHow, friend, should the mind [*citta*] be steadied?
an4.94:3.3: How should the mind [*citta*] be settled?
an4.94:3.4: How should the mind [*citta*] be made unified [*ekodi*]?
an4.94:3.5: How should the mind [*citta*] be brought to *samДҒdhi* [*samДҒdahДҒtabban*]?вҖҷ
an4.94:3.6: He explains it to him according to what he has seen and known:
an4.94:3.7: вҖҳThis is how, friend, the mind [*citta*] should be steadied, this is how the mind [*citta*] should be settled, this is how the mind [*citta*] should be made unified [*ekodi*], this is how the mind [*citta*] should be brought to *samДҒdhi* [*samДҒdahДҒtabban*].вҖҷ
an4.94:3.8: Then after some time he gains both insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*] and inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*].
an4.94:4.1: Now, bhikkhus, as to the person who gains neither inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*] nor insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*], that person should approach the person who gains both inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*] and insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*] and say to him:
an4.94:4.10: вҖҳThis is how, friend, the mind [*citta*] should be steadied, this is how the mind [*citta*] should be settled, this is how the mind [*citta*] should be made unified [*ekodi*], this is how the mind [*citta*] should be brought to *samДҒdhi* [*samДҒdahДҒtabbaб№Ғ*], this is how *saб№…khДҒras* should be seen, this is how *saб№…khДҒras* should be examined, this is how *saб№…khДҒras* should be seen with insight [*vipassitabbДҒ*].вҖҷ
an4.94:4.11: Then after some time he gains both inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*] and insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*].
an4.94:4.2: вҖҳHow, friend, should the mind [*citta*] be steadied?
an4.94:4.3: How should the mind [*citta*] be settled?
an4.94:4.4: How should the mind [*citta*] be made unified [*ekodi*]?
an4.94:4.5: How should the mind [*citta*] be brought to *samДҒdhi* [*samДҒdahДҒtabbaб№Ғ*]?
an4.94:4.8: How should *saб№…khДҒras* be seen with insight [*vipassitabbДҒ*]?вҖҷ
an4.94:4.9: He explains it to him according to what he has seen and known:
an4.94:5.1: Now, bhikkhus, as to the person who gains both inward settling of the mind [*ajjhattaб№Ғ cetosamathassa*] and insight into *dhammas* through higher discernment [*adhipaГұГұДҒdhammavipassanДҒya*], that person, grounded in those very skillful *dhammas*, should make a further effort for the ending of the *ДҒsavas*.
an4.94:5.2: These, bhikkhus, are the four persons who are present and found in the world."
